description: UNet-skip decoder-matched cross-bundle Pearson r and within-FM seed-pair Pearson r per task. Cross-bundle pairs are restricted to within-split (orig-5-cluster and e1-4-expansion use disjoint test splits). Within-FM uses seeds 42 and 43.
method: intersection-aligned per_case_dice from per_case_dice_m4 (orig 5 backbones) and per_case_dice_m4_e1/per_case_dice_m4 (4 new backbones)

riga_cup.note: 32 cross-bundle pairs because cross-bundle pairs are restricted to within-split where backbones share identical test IDs; the original-5 cluster and e1-4 expansion encode the same RIGA test cases differently (hashes vs paths), so cross-cluster pairs cannot be directly aligned without a hash↔path map.

brats_wt.note: BraTS column NA for 4 new backbones (data layout); only orig-5 backbones evaluated

interpretation: Decoder-matched UNet-skip same-vs-cross gaps are 14%-68% of the 1x1-conv primary post-floor gaps (mean retention ratio gap_unetskip/gap_1x1 = 0.32; equivalently mean attenuation = 68%). The same/cross gap shrinks substantially when the cross-bundle reference is computed under the matched UNet-skip decoder rather than the 1x1-conv decoder.
